description_long: This object consists of two large, orange-capped mushrooms growing from a small patch of grassy earth. The mushroom caps are broad and slightly domed, with subtle, irregular lighter patches and a matte, somewhat wrinkled surface. The stems are thick, pale beige with faint grayish spots, and gently taper toward the ground. The base is surrounded by tufts of green grass and several small, gray rocks, giving a naturalistic appearance. The larger mushroom stands taller and more centrally, while the smaller one is nestled to the side, partially overlapping the first. The overall scene evokes a whimsical, stylized depiction of mushrooms commonly found in parks or garden settings.
description: A pair of large, orange-capped mushrooms with thick stems, growing from a grassy patch with rocks and foliage. The caps are broad and matte, with subtle lighter markings.
description_view_0: The larger mushroom is prominent, showing its full cap and thick stem, with grass and rocks visible at the base.
description_view_1: Both mushrooms are visible, with the smaller one clearly seen to the right and slightly behind the larger cap.
description_view_2: The view highlights the underside of the smaller mushroom cap and the overlapping arrangement of the two mushrooms.
description_view_3: The smaller mushroom is more forward, with both stems and the surrounding grass and rocks clearly visible.
attribute_cap_pattern: matte with subtle irregular lighter patches
